name: Bédar Mining Route Loop
name_original: Ruta de la Mina — circular desde Bédar — Llanos del Garrapancho
description: Explore the Ruta de la Mina, a moderate 19.2-mile mountain biking trail from Bédar, featuring historic mining sites and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Bédar offers routes through the foothills of the Sierra de los Filabres mountains, situated approximately 400 meters above sea level. The region features mountainous terrain with panoramic views of the Mediterranean coast and green valleys. A network of trails, including historic mining routes, caters to various skill levels. The landscape is characterized by olive groves, almond trees, and citrus orchards. Best mountain bike trails around Bédar The most popular mountain bike trail is Ruta de la Mina — circular desde Bédar — Llanos del Garrapancho, a 19.2 miles (30.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route reflects the region's industrial past. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mojácar Playa Promenade – Macenas Castle loop from Los Tres Amigos, a difficult 43.1 miles (69.3 km) path. This route offers extensive coastal views and challenging terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Mojácar Playa Promenade – Garrucha loop from Los Gallardos, a 34.8 miles (55.9 km) trail leading through coastal areas and agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Bédar is defined by mountainous terrain, historic mining routes, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Bédar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bédar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on mountain bike trails around Bédar?
A: The mountain bike trails around Bédar offer varied terrain, from easy routes suitable for all fitness levels to moderate and difficult paths. You'll encounter mountainous landscapes, historic mining routes, and agricultural areas with olive groves and almond trees. The region's elevated position provides stunning panoramic views of the Mediterranean coast and green valleys. There are approximately 12 easy, 24 moderate, and 16 difficult routes available.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Bédar?
A: Yes, Bédar offers several mountain bike trails suitable for beginners and families. There are about 12 easy routes that cater to all fitness levels, allowing you to enjoy the scenic landscapes without overly challenging climbs or technical sections. These routes are perfect for a more relaxed ride through the region's charming countryside.

Q: What natural features or historical sights can I see while mountain biking in Bédar?
A: Mountain biking in Bédar allows you to explore a rich blend of natural beauty and historical heritage. You can discover remnants of the 19th-century mining boom, such as old mine shafts and former railway tracks, particularly along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031299432/ruta-de-la-mina-circular-desde-bedar-llanos-del-garrapancho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Mina&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable features include the restored Fuente Temprana natural spring water fountain and the Lavadero public wash house, which are part of the 'Routa del Agua'. You'll also pass through picturesque olive groves and almond orchards, and enjoy breathtaking views of the Mediterranean coast from various viewpoints, such as the San Gregorio Square. Nearby attractions include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Old Town&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3581240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Mora of Mojácar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Bédar?
A: Yes, there are circular mountain bike routes in Bédar. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031299432/ruta-de-la-mina-circular-desde-bedar-llanos-del-garrapancho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Mina — circular desde Bédar — Llanos del Garrapancho&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 19.2-mile (30.9 km) trail that takes approximately 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route offers a great way to experience the region's mining history and scenic views in a loop.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Bédar?
A: The mountain bike trails in Bédar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse mountainous terrain, the historical mining routes, and the stunning panoramic views of the Mediterranean coast. The variety of routes, from easy to difficult, also makes it a popular destination for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Bédar offers several challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders. There are approximately 16 difficult routes that require good to very good fitness and advanced riding skills. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38267597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Playa Promenade – Macenas Castle loop from Los Tres Amigos&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 43.1-mile (69.3 km) path with significant elevation changes, offering extensive coastal views and technical sections.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Bédar?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, Bédar's location in Almería, Spain, suggests that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are typically mild, but some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. Bédar's elevated position provides excellent opportunities for coastal views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38267597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Playa Promenade – Macenas Castle loop from Los Tres Amigos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38266495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Playa Promenade – Garrucha loop from Los Gallardos&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive vistas of the Mediterranean Sea and the coastline, combining mountainous terrain with beautiful sea backdrops.

Q: How long do mountain bike routes around Bédar typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike routes around Bédar varies significantly depending on their length and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031299432/ruta-de-la-mina-circular-desde-bedar-llanos-del-garrapancho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Mina&lt;/a&gt; (19.2 miles / 30.9 km) typically takes around 2 hours 40 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carrasca – Fountain loop from Los Gallardos&lt;/a&gt; (21.6 miles / 34.7 km), can take over 2 hours 50 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38267597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Playa Promenade – Macenas Castle loop&lt;/a&gt; (43.1 miles / 69.3 km) might take over 5 hours 50 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific water features or springs to look out for on the trails?
A: Yes, the region has notable water features. The 'Routa del Agua Bedar' specifically highlights the restored Fuente Temprana natural spring water fountain, also known as Alfaquira Alfaguara, and the Lavadero public wash house. These sites offer a glimpse into the area's agricultural history and traditional water management, and can be found along certain trails.

Q: What is the average elevation gain on mountain bike trails in Bédar?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly across the trails. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031299432/ruta-de-la-mina-circular-desde-bedar-llanos-del-garrapancho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Mina&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of around 583 meters. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38267597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Playa Promenade – Macenas Castle loop&lt;/a&gt;, can feature substantial climbs, with an elevation gain of approximately 1473 meters, reflecting the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de los Filabres foothills.
tips: Route along the old route of the Besar mines to Garrucha, with sections in poor condition, but totally cyclable and with fantastic views.
Spectacular path that runs along the Ruta de la Mina, entering its caves. Important to watch with the height of the caves, which do not exceed 1.30 m.

faqs.question: Where does the Ruta de la Mina trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Bédar, specifically from the Llanos del Garrapancho area. While the exact trailhead isn't specified, Bédar is the primary starting point. Parking is generally available in and around the village of Bédar, which serves as the base for this popular route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses varied mountainous terrain. You'll encounter a mix of tracks, paths, and roads. Some sections follow old railway lines, and you might find parts with sand and larger stones. The ground inside tunnels can be uneven. While generally cyclable, be aware that some segments might be in poor condition. You'll ride through agricultural landscapes, including olive groves, almond trees, and citrus orchards.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and historical features can I expect along the Ruta de la Mina?
faqs.answer: You can expect extensive panoramic views, including the Mediterranean coast and lush green valleys. A significant part of the scenery comes from its historical context, with remnants of old mining installations like loading docks, engine sheds, and hoppers. You'll also pass through arid mining landscapes and picturesque villages with white houses. Don't miss the View of the Old Mine on the Greenway and the Ruta de la Mina Trail itself, which is a highlight.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de la Mina suitable for beginners, or is it more challenging?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it requires good fitness and some sections might be demanding with steep inclines, sources suggest it can be suitable for various skill levels. Some parts might be easier (S0-S1 on the Single Trail Scale), but overall, it corresponds to an S2 rating, implying a need for good fitness and advanced riding skills for comfortable completion.

faqs.question: What are the notable features or specific highlights to look out for?
faqs.answer: A unique aspect of this route is its integration of the region's industrial past. You'll encounter various remnants of the old mining industry, including former railway lines, tunnels, and old mining structures. A particular highlight is the inclusion of tunnels, such as the Servalico tunnel, which stretches over 200 meters. There are also spectacular vistas from viewpoints like Hoyo Júpiter.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the Mediterranean climate and arid landscapes, spring and autumn are generally the best times to ride this route. The temperatures are milder, making for a more comfortable experience. Summers can be very hot, and while winters are mild, some sections might be affected by rain.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails. You will ride along parts of the Ruta de la Minería de Bédar and the Sendero Minero Los Gallardos, adding to the historical and scenic diversity of your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de la Mina dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there's no explicit restriction mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the varied terrain, potential for other trail users, and agricultural areas. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and can handle the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to access the Ruta de la Mina. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety tips or gear recommendations for this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to the tunnels on the route, such as the Servalico tunnel which is over 200 meters long, it is essential to bring a flashlight or headlamp. The ground inside these tunnels can be uneven and dark. Also, be prepared for varied terrain, including potentially poor sections, and ensure your bike is in good condition. Good fitness is recommended for the moderate difficulty.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and ends in Bédar, you'll find local amenities within the village. Bédar and nearby areas in Almería offer cafes, pubs, and various accommodation options, making it convenient for pre- or post-ride refreshments and stays.

waypoints.name: View of the Old Mine on the Greenway
